scope.summary: Non-JS async runtime obligation attribution now requires stronger runtime identity evidence before Python asyncio, Rust spawn/aggregate macro, or Swift Task surfaces receive shared scheduling/task/aggregate labels.

capability_alignment.capability: runtime API identity proof before obligation attribution
not_a_feature_list: No new SourceProtocolKind, SourceCallKind, or public semantic-pack API was added.
No exact admission path was opened for Python, Rust, or Swift async runtime APIs.
Python asyncio aliases remain outside this slice until alias-backed occurrence proof is modeled.
Rust unqualified join!/try_join!/select! macros are no longer priced or reported as runtime aggregates by name alone.
reused_capabilities: Python import namespace binding evidence and local module path checks
Rust same-file runtime root definition checks
Swift local and corpus-visible definition/shadow detection
shared task and async aggregate obligation labels

guarded_surfaces.guard: The receiver must be the import-backed asyncio namespace, with no same-file asyncio parameter or assignment shadow and no path-visible local asyncio module.
blocked_false_open_examples: asyncio.py plus import asyncio in a sibling or child module

guarded_surfaces.guard: Rust async runtime reporting is limited to qualified tokio/async_std/futures/futures_util paths whose root is not locally defined in the same file; aggregate rows must also be macro invocations.
Task reporting requires the Task root not to be shadowed by a same-file definition or by corpus-visible Swift Task definitions.
blocked_false_open_examples: Task.swift defining struct Task plus another file calling Task { work() }

assertions: shadowed Python asyncio.gather does not report async-aggregate-all-completion-contract
unimported Python asyncio.sleep does not report timer-scheduling-contract
unqualified Rust join! does not report async-aggregate-all-completion-contract
unqualified Rust select! does not report async-aggregate-first-completion-contract
shadowed Swift Task and Task.detached do not report task-spawn-scheduling-contract
path-visible local Python asyncio.py blocks task-spawn-scheduling-contract
same-file local Rust mod tokio blocks task-spawn-scheduling-contract
same-file local Rust mod tokio blocks async-aggregate-all-completion-contract
corpus-visible Swift Task type blocks task-spawn-scheduling-contract
unrelated Python asyncio.py does not block stdlib asyncio attribution
unrelated Rust mod tokio does not block qualified tokio attribution

corpus_pricing_delta.reason: The audit now prices only qualified tokio/futures/futures_util aggregate macros, matching the runtime-boundary reporter. Rust spawn rows were already qualified and are now guarded against same-file local runtime roots.

next_exact_work_policy.summary: The reporter now rejects common shadowed or unqualified false runtime identities. Exact recovery still requires proof for scheduling, cancellation, lifecycle, result-channel, exception, and runtime-specific callback/effect behavior.
